DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DENNIS G. COSSO, ESQ. of Law Office of Dennis G. Cosso, Tel No. 626-574-8000 appearing telephonically for Plaintiff;
Atty. PATRICK J. WHITEHORN, ESQ. of Last & Faoro, Tel No. 650-696-8350 appearing for Defendant Mint Development, L.P.;
Atty. MOLLY E. HEALY, ESQ. of Gibbs, Giden, Locher, Turner, Senet & Wittbrodt LLP, Tel No. 310-734-3384 appearing for Cross-Defendant Layton Construction Company.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT MINT DEVELOPMENT, L.P.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendant Mint Development's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for implied contract is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Kingspan is granted leave to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that Mint has not paid for some or all of the labor and materials Kingspan provided to Mint's property. But for the failure to allege that Mint has not paid for Kingspan's work, the fifth cause of action sufficiently alleges a claim for quantum meruit notwithstanding that there is no privity between Mint and Kingspan. Quantum meruit claims were rejected in Truestone and Rogers, the two decisions relied on by Mint, not because of lack of privity, but because the defendants had paid for the work performed by the plaintiff. (De Laurentis Entertainment Group Inc. v. National Broadcasting Co. (9th Cir. 1992) 963 Fe.2d 1269, 1273, n.6). Kingspan's allegation that Mint requested Kingspan's panels, if coupled with the further allegation that Mint didn't pay for Kingspan's work, is sufficient for a quantum meruit claim. Kingspan's request for judicial notice is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
